items.fromString:
Verifies that signed-in MAC Pro members see Talon.One Pro pricing (with the regular price struck through) on product cards, PDP, search results, mini cart, and the cart page, that signed-out/non-member customers always see standard pricing, and that the feature can be toggled off from the theme editor.

h2. Prerequisites

* A test customer account that is a confirmed MAC Pro member in Talon.One (status Active, a member level assigned — e.g. Level A gets 40% off). Ask the team for current test credentials if you don't already have one, or set one up per "Setting up a test member" below.
* Pro-eligible products for PDP / card / search checks — the same ones as the PR preview links: Studio Fix 36HR Smooth Angles Concealer {{/products/studio-fix-36hr-smooth-angles-concealer}} (shade picker, $30.00 → $18.00 for a Level-A member) and Studio Radiance Serum-Powered Foundation {{/products/studio-radiance-serum-powered™-foundation}} ($46.00 → $27.60).
* Search queries that reliably surface them on the search page: {{studio fix 36hr}} (concealer) and {{studio radiance serum}} (foundation).
* One *in-stock* Pro-eligible item for cart/drawer/subtotal checks (Pro pricing can only be exercised in a cart if the item can actually be added) — an in-stock shade of the concealer above works, e.g. *NC40* ($30.00 → $18.00); 209 Synthetic Eye Liner Brush {{/products/209-synthetic-eye-liner-brush-m45f01}} ($25.00 → $15.00) is an alternative.
* Non-eligible contrast: on the dev store, Concealer shade *NC30* and Foundation shade *NC20* currently have their Talon.One pricing data removed — click them in the shade picker next to any other shade to see standard vs Pro side-by-side (temporary demo setup; may be restored later). A product with no promo data at all also works (M·A·C Lash 7 Free Spirit Lash {{/products/m-a-c-lash-7-free-spirit-lash}}, $16.00, never discounts).
* Theme setting location: Online Store → Customize → Theme settings → Talon.One section.

h3. Setting up a test member (makes Talon.One write the pricing data)

Pro pricing renders only after Talon.One writes the {{talon_one.data}} customer metafield (member level and status live inside it). To turn a dev-store customer into a test member:

# In Shopify admin → Customers → open the customer → *Metafields*: set *Member Type* = {{MAC PRO}}, *Member Level* = {{A}} (or the tier you're testing), *Member Status* = {{Active}}.
# In the customer's native *Notes* field (the plain Notes box on the customer page — NOT a metafield): enter {{1}} and save. This enrolls the customer as a loyalty member in Talon.One.
# In Talon.One: *Customers* → open the profile → *Loyalty Points* → click the *[…]* button next to the Ledger dropdown → *Add points*; in the *Reason* field type {{RedeemablePoints}}; add at least 1 point.
# Wait for the connector to write the {{talon_one.data}} metafield — a few seconds up to ~10 minutes. Then sign in on the storefront as this customer and Pro prices appear.

h2. 1. Signed-out customer always sees standard pricing

# In an incognito/private window, open a Pro-eligible product's PDP.
# Expected: regular price only, no strikethrough price, no "MAC Pro price" (or similar) label anywhere on the page.
# Open a collection/listing page containing that product, and search for it by keyword.
# Expected: the same standard price on every card, no Pro styling.
# Add the in-stock eligible product to the cart and open the cart drawer.
# Expected: standard line price, no strike, no Pro label; drawer subtotal equals the standard price.

h2. 2. Signed-in MAC Pro member sees Pro pricing everywhere a price shows

# Sign in as the confirmed MAC Pro member.
# Open the eligible product's PDP. Expected: the discounted Pro price shows in the main price slot, and the regular price shows struck through next to it (e.g. the concealer shows $18.00 with $30.00 struck through for a Level-A member).
# Open a collection/listing page containing the product. Expected: the card shows the same Pro price + struck-through regular price; card layout is otherwise unaffected.
# Search for the product by keyword. Expected: the search-result card shows the same Pro price + struck-through regular price.
# Add the in-stock eligible product to the cart and open the cart drawer. Expected: the line shows the Pro price + struck-through original price with a "MAC Pro price" (or similar) label; the drawer subtotal reflects the Pro price.
# Navigate to the standalone cart page ({{/cart}}). Expected: the same line still shows the struck-through original + Pro price, and the page subtotal reflects Pro pricing — this page is fully server-rendered, so there should be no visible flash or change after the page loads.

h2. 3. Non-eligible products and non-member customers stay at standard price

# Still signed in as the Pro member, open a product/shade that has no Talon.One promotional pricing data (e.g. the lash, or the NC30 / NC20 demo shades from Prerequisites). Expected: standard price, no strikethrough, no Pro label.
# If a product is known to carry only a stale/expired promotion entry, open its PDP. Expected: standard price — a stale/removed promotion must never render a discount.
# If a second test account is available that is signed in but NOT a MAC Pro member (no member level, or status not Active), sign in as that account and repeat step 2 of section 2. Expected: standard pricing throughout, identical to signed-out.

h2. 4. Pricing updates immediately on sign-in / sign-out

# As the Pro member, add the in-stock eligible product to the cart so the drawer shows a Pro-priced line and Pro subtotal.
# Sign out without emptying the cart.
# Reload the cart page (or reopen the drawer). Expected: the same cart line now shows the standard price (no strike, no Pro label), and the subtotal recalculates to the standard total.
# Sign back in as the same Pro member without re-adding the item. Expected: the cart line and subtotal return to Pro pricing.

h2. 5. Mixed cart — only eligible items get Pro pricing

# As the Pro member, add both the in-stock eligible product and a non-eligible product to the cart.
# Open the cart drawer. Expected: the eligible line shows the Pro price + struck-through original + Pro label; the non-eligible line shows its standard price with no Pro styling.
# Expected subtotal: sum of the Pro-discounted eligible line + the standard non-eligible line (e.g. concealer NC40 at $18.00 + the lash at $16.00 = $34.00).
# Repeat on the standalone {{/cart}} page. Expected: same per-line behavior and the same subtotal.

h2. 6. Admin display configuration

# Go to Online Store → Customize → Theme settings → Talon.One.
# Confirm an "Enable Pro pricing" checkbox is present and defaults to ON.
# With the Pro member signed in and viewing the eligible PDP, turn "Enable Pro pricing" OFF and save/preview.
# Expected: the product now shows only the standard price everywhere (PDP, cards, drawer) — Pro pricing is fully suppressed even though the customer still qualifies.
# Turn "Enable Pro pricing" back ON to restore the default.
# Locate the existing "show discount percentage" display setting and turn it ON.
# Expected: on the eligible PDP, a percentage-off badge appears alongside the Pro price (e.g. "40% off").
# Restore the discount-percentage setting to its original value.
# Confirm eligibility and discount logic themselves are not configurable in the theme — both are managed entirely in Talon.One; the theme only controls the display (label, original-price visibility, strikethrough visibility).

h2. 7. Accessibility

# On the eligible PDP as the Pro member, inspect the price region with a screen reader (or browser accessibility tree). Expected: the discounted Pro price and the regular price are each announced with a clear, distinguishing label (e.g. "MAC Pro price" vs. "Regular price") in a logical reading order, so a screen-reader user cannot confuse which price is which.
# Repeat the check on a collection card and in the cart drawer.
# Visually confirm the strikethrough price and Pro/discount labels meet the same color contrast as the site's existing sale pricing treatment.
# Zoom the browser to 200% on the PDP, a collection card, and the cart drawer. Expected: price, strikethrough, and Pro label remain fully visible with no clipped/overlapping text, and add-to-cart and other functionality stay usable.
# Where the browser/assistive tech supports live-region announcements, verify a price change (e.g. after sign-out) is announced or at least immediately reflected in the accessibility tree.

* A test customer account that is a confirmed MAC Pro member in Talon.One (status Active, a member level assigned — e.g. Level A gets 40% off). Ask the team for current test credentials if you don't already have one, or set one up per "Setting up a test member" below.
* One Pro-eligible product to use for PDP / card / search checks — ask the team for a current example (during development, MAC Of All Trades Brush Kit {{/products/mac-of-all-trades-brush-kit-s66dy5}} went from $99.00 to $59.40 for a Level-A member).
* One *in-stock* Pro-eligible product for cart/drawer/subtotal checks (Pro pricing can only be exercised in a cart if the item can actually be added — during development, 209 Synthetic Eye Liner Brush {{/products/209-synthetic-eye-liner-brush-m45f01}} went from $25.00 to $15.00 for a Level-A member).
* One non-eligible / standard product for contrast (during development, M·A·C Lash 7 Free Spirit Lash {{/products/m-a-c-lash-7-free-spirit-lash}}, $16.00, never discounts).
* Theme setting location: Online Store → Customize → Theme settings → Talon.One section.

h3. Setting up a test member (makes Talon.One write the pricing data)

Pro pricing renders only after Talon.One writes the {{talon_one.data}} customer metafield (member level and status live inside it). To turn a dev-store customer into a test member:

# In Shopify admin → Customers → open the customer → *Metafields*: set *Member Type* = {{MAC PRO}}, *Member Level* = {{A}} (or the tier you're testing), *Member Status* = {{Active}}.
# In the customer's native *Notes* field (the plain Notes box on the customer page — NOT a metafield): enter {{1}} and save. This enrolls the customer as a loyalty member in Talon.One.
# In Talon.One: *Customers* → open the profile → *Loyalty Points* → click the *[…]* button next to the Ledger dropdown → *Add points*; in the *Reason* field type {{RedeemablePoints}}; add at least 1 point.
# Wait for the connector to write the {{talon_one.data}} metafield — a few seconds up to ~10 minutes. Then sign in on the storefront as this customer and Pro prices appear.

h2. 1. Signed-out customer always sees standard pricing

# In an incognito/private window, open a Pro-eligible product's PDP.
# Expected: regular price only, no strikethrough price, no "MAC Pro price" (or similar) label anywhere on the page.
# Open a collection/listing page containing that product, and search for it by keyword.
# Expected: the same standard price on every card, no Pro styling.
# Add the in-stock eligible product to the cart and open the cart drawer.
# Expected: standard line price, no strike, no Pro label; drawer subtotal equals the standard price.

h2. 2. Signed-in MAC Pro member sees Pro pricing everywhere a price shows

# Sign in as the confirmed MAC Pro member.
# Open the eligible product's PDP. Expected: the discounted Pro price shows in the main price slot, and the regular price shows struck through next to it (e.g. $59.40 with $99.00 struck through).
# Open a collection/listing page containing the product. Expected: the card shows the same Pro price + struck-through regular price; card layout is otherwise unaffected.
# Search for the product by keyword. Expected: the search-result card shows the same Pro price + struck-through regular price.
# Add the in-stock eligible product to the cart and open the cart drawer. Expected: the line shows the Pro price + struck-through original price with a "MAC Pro price" (or similar) label; the drawer subtotal reflects the Pro price.
# Navigate to the standalone cart page ({{/cart}}). Expected: the same line still shows the struck-through original + Pro price, and the page subtotal reflects Pro pricing — this page is fully server-rendered, so there should be no visible flash or change after the page loads.

h2. 3. Non-eligible products and non-member customers stay at standard price

# Still signed in as the Pro member, open a product that has no Talon.One promotional pricing data at all (e.g. the lash). Expected: standard price, no strikethrough, no Pro label.
# If a product is known to carry only a stale/expired promotion entry, open its PDP. Expected: standard price — a stale/removed promotion must never render a discount.
# If a second test account is available that is signed in but NOT a MAC Pro member (no member level, or status not Active), sign in as that account and repeat step 2 of section 2. Expected: standard pricing throughout, identical to signed-out.

h2. 4. Pricing updates immediately on sign-in / sign-out

# As the Pro member, add the in-stock eligible product to the cart so the drawer shows a Pro-priced line and Pro subtotal.
# Sign out without emptying the cart.
# Reload the cart page (or reopen the drawer). Expected: the same cart line now shows the standard price (no strike, no Pro label), and the subtotal recalculates to the standard total.
# Sign back in as the same Pro member without re-adding the item. Expected: the cart line and subtotal return to Pro pricing.

h2. 5. Mixed cart — only eligible items get Pro pricing

# As the Pro member, add both the in-stock eligible product and a non-eligible product to the cart.
# Open the cart drawer. Expected: the eligible line shows the Pro price + struck-through original + Pro label; the non-eligible line shows its standard price with no Pro styling.
# Expected subtotal: sum of the Pro-discounted eligible line + the standard non-eligible line (e.g. $15.00 + $25.00 = $40.00).
# Repeat on the standalone {{/cart}} page. Expected: same per-line behavior and the same subtotal.

h2. 6. Admin display configuration

# Go to Online Store → Customize → Theme settings → Talon.One.
# Confirm an "Enable Pro pricing" checkbox is present and defaults to ON.
# With the Pro member signed in and viewing the eligible PDP, turn "Enable Pro pricing" OFF and save/preview.
# Expected: the product now shows only the standard price everywhere (PDP, cards, drawer) — Pro pricing is fully suppressed even though the customer still qualifies.
# Turn "Enable Pro pricing" back ON to restore the default.
# Locate the existing "show discount percentage" display setting and turn it ON.
# Expected: on the eligible PDP, a percentage-off badge appears alongside the Pro price (e.g. "40% off").
# Restore the discount-percentage setting to its original value.
# Confirm eligibility and discount logic themselves are not configurable in the theme — both are managed entirely in Talon.One; the theme only controls the display (label, original-price visibility, strikethrough visibility).

h2. 7. Accessibility

# On the eligible PDP as the Pro member, inspect the price region with a screen reader (or browser accessibility tree). Expected: the discounted Pro price and the regular price are each announced with a clear, distinguishing label (e.g. "MAC Pro price" vs. "Regular price") in a logical reading order, so a screen-reader user cannot confuse which price is which.
# Repeat the check on a collection card and in the cart drawer.
# Visually confirm the strikethrough price and Pro/discount labels meet the same color contrast as the site's existing sale pricing treatment.
# Zoom the browser to 200% on the PDP, a collection card, and the cart drawer. Expected: price, strikethrough, and Pro label remain fully visible with no clipped/overlapping text, and add-to-cart and other functionality stay usable.
# Where the browser/assistive tech supports live-region announcements, verify a price change (e.g. after sign-out) is announced or at least immediately reflected in the accessibility tree.

h2. 8. Responsive check

# Repeat the PDP, card, and drawer checks from section 2 at a mobile width (~390px). Expected: price row, strikethrough, and Pro label remain intact with no overflow or truncation.
# Repeat at a standard desktop width. Expected: same result.

h2. Notes for the tester

* Pro pricing depends on live Talon.One data. If a normally-eligible product unexpectedly shows standard pricing, first confirm its Talon.One promotion/campaign is still active before treating it as a defect.
* Price-display checks (PDP/card/search) work even if a product is out of stock, but cart/drawer/subtotal checks require an in-stock, Pro-eligible product.

User

Pro Pricing Eligibility

# MAC Pro pricing displays only when the customer is signed in and qualifies as a MAC Pro member
# Signed-out customers see standard product pricing
# Signed-in customers who are not MAC Pro members see standard product pricing
# If MAC Pro eligibility cannot be confirmed, standard pricing displays until eligibility is available
# MAC Pro pricing updates after the customer signs in or signs out

Product Pricing Display

# Eligible MAC Pro members see MAC Pro pricing in supported product commerce areas
# Supported areas include:
## Product cards (everywhere a product price is displayed across the site)
## Mini cart
# {color:#bf2600}The regular price can display as a compare or strikethrough price{color}

Cart Display

# Eligible MAC Pro members see MAC Pro pricing reflected on applicable cart line items
## The cart line item displays the discounted Pro price when the product qualifies
## {color:#bf2600}The cart line item displays the original price and applied discount{color}
# MAC Pro discounts are reflected in cart subtotal and discount calculations
# If a cart contains both Pro-eligible and non-Pro-eligible products, only eligible products receive Pro pricing
# If a customer signs out or no longer qualifies, Pro pricing is removed and the cart returns to standard pricing

Promotion Eligibility

# MAC Pro member eligibility is determined solely by Talon.One
# If a MAC Pro member is eligible for additional promotions, promotion stacking follows the configured Talon.One rules

Accessibility

# Pricing is available to screen reader users in a meaningful order
# Discounted price, original price, and {color:#bf2600}discount labels{color} are communicated clearly to assistive technology
# Pricing changes after sign-in or cart updates are communicated where supported
# Text, price treatments, strikethroughs, and labels meet minimum color contrast requirements
# Pricing display supports browser zoom up to 200% without loss of content or functionality

1. Signed-out customer always sees standard pricing
In an incognito/private window, open a Pro-eligible product's PDP.
Expected: regular price only, no strikethrough price, no "MAC Pro price" (or similar) label anywhere on the page.
Open a collection/listing page containing that product, and search for it by keyword.
Expected: the same standard price on every card, no Pro styling.
Add the in-stock eligible product to the cart and open the cart drawer.
Expected: standard line price, no strike, no Pro label; drawer subtotal equals the standard price.
2. Signed-in MAC Pro member sees Pro pricing everywhere a price shows
Sign in as the confirmed MAC Pro member.

Open the eligible product's PDP. Expected: the discounted Pro price shows in the main price slot, and the regular price shows struck through next to it (e.g. the concealer shows $18.00 with $30.00 struck through for a Level-A member).
Open a collection/listing page containing the product. Expected: the card shows the same Pro price + struck-through regular price; card layout is otherwise unaffected.
Search for the product by keyword. Expected: the search-result card shows the same Pro price + struck-through regular price.

Add the in-stock eligible product to the cart and open the cart drawer. Expected: the line shows the Pro price + struck-through original price with a "MAC Pro price" (or similar) label; the drawer subtotal reflects the Pro price.
Navigate to the standalone cart page (

). Expected: the same line still shows the struck-through original + Pro price, and the page subtotal reflects Pro pricing — this page is fully server-rendered, so there should be no visible flash or change after the page loads.
3. Non-eligible products and non-member customers stay at standard price
Still signed in as the Pro member, open a product/shade that has no Talon.One promotional pricing data. Expected: standard price, no strikethrough, no Pro label.
If a product is known to carry only a stale/expired promotion entry, open its PDP. Expected: standard price — a stale/removed promotion must never render a discount.

If a second test account is available that is signed in but NOT a MAC Pro member (no member level, or status not Active), sign in as that account and repeat step 2 of section 2. Expected: standard pricing throughout, identical to signed-out.
4. Pricing updates immediately on sign-in / sign-out
As the Pro member, add the in-stock eligible product to the cart so the drawer shows a Pro-priced line and Pro subtotal.
Sign out without emptying the cart.
Reload the cart page (or reopen the drawer). Expected: the same cart line now shows the standard price (no strike, no Pro label), and the subtotal recalculates to the standard total.
Sign back in as the same Pro member without re-adding the item. Expected: the cart line and subtotal return to Pro pricing.
5. Mixed cart — only eligible items get Pro pricing
As the Pro member, add both the in-stock eligible product and a non-eligible product to the cart.
Open the cart drawer. Expected: the eligible line shows the Pro price + struck-through original + Pro label; the non-eligible line shows its standard price with no Pro styling.
Expected subtotal: sum of the Pro-discounted eligible line + the standard non-eligible line (e.g. concealer NC40 at $18.00 + the lash at $16.00 = $34.00).
page. Expected: same per-line behavior and the same subtotal.
